sources.title: No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day
sources.author: GeForce NOW Community
sources.summary: Head straight for orbit with GeForce NOW — no space helmet required. PRAGMATA, Capcom’s long-awaited sci-fi action adventure, touches down on GeForce NOW the same day it launches worldwide. The futuristic journey through a cold lunar station in the near future can be streamed instantly from the cloud to almost any device, no console or […]
sources.content_text: ![country_code](https://www.nvidia.com/content/dam/1x1-00000000.png) [Skip to content](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/geforce-now-thursday-pragmata/#primary) # No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day Stream some out-of-this-world adventures with five new games joining the cloud; plus, GeForce NOW comes to India for the first time starting today. April 16, 2026 by [GeForce NOW Community](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/author/geforcenowcommunity/ "View all posts by GeForce NOW Community") [0](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/geforce-now-thursday-pragmata/#disqus_thread) Share Share This Article * [ X ](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=No%20Need%20for%20Space%20Gear%20%E2%80%94%20Capcom%E2%80%99s%20%E2%80%98PRAGMATA%E2%80%99%20Joins%20GeForce%20NOW%20on%20Launch%20Day%20https%3A%2F%2Fblogs.nvidia.com%2Fblog%2Fgeforce-now-thursday-pragmata%2F) * [ Facebook ](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fblogs.nvidia.com%2Fblog%2Fgeforce-now-thursday-pragmata%2F) * [ LinkedIn ](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https%3A%2F%2Fblogs.nvidia.com%2Fblog%2Fgeforce-now-thursday-pragmata%2F&title=GFN+Thursday%3A+%E2%80%98PRAGMATA%E2%80%99+on+GeForce+NOW+%7C+NVIDIA+Blog) * [ Copy link Link copied! ](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/geforce-now-thursday-pragmata/ "Copy link to clipboard") ![PRAGMATA on GeForce NOW](https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/GFN_Thursday-PRAGMATA-1280x640.jpg) Head straight for orbit with [GeForce NOW](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/geforce-now/) — no space helmet required. _PRAGMATA_ , Capcom’s long-awaited sci-fi action adventure, touches down on GeForce NOW the same day it launches worldwide. The futuristic journey through a cold lunar station in the near future can be streamed instantly from the cloud to almost any device, no console or heavy hardware needed. That’s only the beginning. Five new titles join the cloud this week, expanding April’s gaming galaxy with fresh adventures and endless possibilities _._ Plus, the GeForce NOW Ultimate membership [comes to gamers in India](https://x.com/NVIDIAGFN/status/2043894514570379303) for the first time, with the service now available in beta and operated by NVIDIA. Time to see what’s landing on GeForce NOW. ## **A Mission Gone Wrong** _PRAGMATA_ is Capcom’s newest sci-fi action adventure that blends heart, high-tech and a hauntingly quiet world set in the near future. Step into the boots of Hugh Williams, an investigator navigating a lunar research station gone silent and Diana, a young android. Armed with an arsenal of weapons and the ability to hack, every corridor and console becomes part of a cinematic experience filled with tense exploration and fast-paced action. The story unfolds amid the cold vacuum of the moon after a massive quake hits the station researching Lunafilament — a material said to be able to create anything given enough data. Awake, injured and disoriented, Hugh crosses paths with Diana, the mysterious android girl known as a Pragmata. Now, they must work together as they face the rogue station on their way back to Earth. _PRAGMATA_ shines in stunning clarity with ray-traced lighting and [NVIDIA DLSS 4](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/geforce/technologies/dlss/) technology boosting frame rates and image quality. Stream it on launch day at full fidelity, even without the latest hardware — no need to wait on a large install or worry about hardware specs. Hugh and Diana’s lunar mystery is ready when the moment strikes. ## **Let’s Play Today** ![fortnite save the world on geforce now](https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/GFN_Thursday-Fornite_Save_The_World-1680x840.jpg)_Heroes in the cloud don’t have to wait for updates._ _Fortnite: Save the World_ is now free and ready to stream instantly on GeForce NOW. The storm hits hard and the heroes hit harder — jump into a co-op adventure that mixes base-building, looting and all-out action against waves of Husks. Craft the ultimate fort, set sneaky traps and team up to protect what’s left of the world — no waiting for updates or patches, just pure fight-and-build mayhem. The storm’s closing in, but thanks to the cloud, the party’s jumping right into the action. “Save the World” [isn’t available on mobile devices](https://nvidia.custhelp.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/5235/), including tablets. In addition, members can look for the following: * _REPLACED_(New release on [Steam](https://store.steampowered.com/app/1663850/REPLACED/) and [Xbox](https://www.xbox.com/en-us/games/store/replaced/9nv3l234vgxd), available on Game Pass, April 14, GeForce RTX 5080-ready) * _Windrose_(New release on [Steam](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3041230/Windrose/), April 14, GeForce RTX 5080-ready) * _Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss_(New release on [Steam](https://store.steampowered.com/app/2760560/Cthulhu_The_Cosmic_Abyss/), April 16, GeForce RTX 5080-ready) * _PRAGMATA_(New release on [Steam](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3357650?utm_source=nvidia&utm_campaign=geforce_now), April 16, GeForce RTX 5080-ready) * _PRAGMATA SKETCHBOOK – DEMO_ ([Steam](https://store.steampowered.com/app/4003800?utm_source=nvidia&utm_campaign=geforce_now), GeForce RTX 5080-ready) What are you planning to play this weekend? Let us know on [X](https://www.twitter.com/nvidiagfn) or in the comments below. > You’re loading into a game and can invite ONE person to your squad… > Who’s your pick? > — ![🌩️](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f329.svg) NVIDIA GeForce NOW (@NVIDIAGFN) [April 15, 2026](https://twitter.com/NVIDIAGFN/status/2044445644815818811?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw) ![Explore the Best of GTC 2026 Sessions](https://blogs.nvidia.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/gtc26-best-of-kit-corp-blog-sidebar-1920x1080-1-960x540.jpg) ### [ Explore the Best of GTC 2026 Sessions ](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/on-demand/search/?facet.event_name%5B%5D=GTC%20San%20Jose&facet.event_year%5B%5D=2026&facet.mimetype%5B%5D=event%20sessio

sources.title: Australian federal court warns lawyers over ‘unacceptable’ use of AI
sources.author: Josh Taylor Technology reporter
sources.summary:
New guidance to legal profession ‘embraces’ use of technology but flags penalties for lawyers who ‘mislead the court’ with AI-generated errors

  • Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates

  • Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast

The federal court of Australia has warned the legal profession about the dangers of using generative artificial intelligence in legal proceedings, issuing new rules for its use, with potential financial or legal consequences if AI errors frustrate court cases.

Amid an explosion in court filings in Australia and across the globe

sources.title: Google to punish sites that trap people in with back button tricks
sources.summary: The tech giant said it will punish sites that block back button navigation from June.
sources.content_text: [Skip to content](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c05dd2yj3z3o#bbc-main) [Watch Live](https://www.bbc.com/watch-live-news/) [British Broadcasting Corporation](https://www.bbc.com/) Subscribe [Sign In](https://session.bbc.com/session?userOrigin=BBCS_BBC&ptrt=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.bbc.com%2Fnews%2Farticles%2Fc05dd2yj3z3o) * [Home](https://www.bbc.com/) * [News](https://www.bbc.com/news) * [Sport](https://www.bbc.com/sport) * [Business](https://www.bbc.com/business) * [Technology](https://www.bbc.com/technology) * [Health](https://www.bbc.com/health) * [Culture](https://www.bbc.com/culture) * [Arts](https://www.bbc.com/arts) * [Travel](https://www.bbc.com/travel) * [Earth](https://www.bbc.com/future-planet) * [Audio](https://www.bbc.com/audio) * [Video](https://www.bbc.com/video) * [Live](https://www.bbc.com/live) * [Documentaries](https://www.bbc.com/video/docs) [Home](https://www.bbc.com/) News [Sport](https://www.bbc.com/sport) Business Technology [Health](https://www.bbc.com/health) Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Documentaries [Weather](https://www.bbc.com/weather) [Newsletters](https://www.bbc.com/newsletters) [Watch Live](https://www.bbc.com/watch-live-news/) # Google to punish sites that trap people in with back button tricks 2 days ago Share Save [Add as preferred on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=bbc.com) Laura CressTechnology reporter ![Getty Images A close up of a woman wearing a green blouse typing on a grey laptop](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/480/cpsprodpb/36f9/live/c3f2a8f0-38a7-11f1-bd3c-df67f5a99d2a.jpg.webp)Getty Images Google says it is expanding its policies to crack down on websites which trap users with "back button hijacking". Back button hijacking is when a website interferes with a browser so the back button no longer takes users to the previous page, instead often keeping them on the site or presenting unsolicited ads. In a blog post the tech giant behind the Chrome browser said it had seen a "rise of this type of behaviour" which had led it to act. From 15 June the tactic will be deemed a "malicious practice", meaning sites which continue to adopt it may be down-ranked or even removed from Google Search results. "Back button hijacking interferes with the browser's functionality, breaks the expected user journey, and results in user frustration," Google [said in its post](https://developers.google.com/search/blog/2026/04/back-button-hijacking). "People report feeling manipulated and eventually less willing to visit unfamiliar sites," it added. Examples of practices it would clamp down on included sites using any technique which inserted "manipulative" pages into a user's browser history that stopped them from returning to the previous page. Adam Thompson, director of digital at BCS, the Chartered Institute for IT, told the BBC: "Practices like back button hijacking undermine the basic user experience and break the expectations people have of how the web should work, so it's understandable that Google views this as a harmful behaviour and [is] taking action." Google advised site owners which did not want to face the new penalties to ensure they did not do "anything to interfere with a user's ability to navigate their browser history", urging them to "thoroughly review their technical implementation". It added sites which were penalised but then fixed the issue could submit a request to Google to have the demotion reconsidered. ## [Businesses scramble to get noticed by AI search](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/c70n2rjgxeyo#:~:text=More%20Technology%20of%20Business%20*%2010%20March.,at%20festivals%20and%20sports%20matches.%2027%20February.) ## [Google launches new AI search feature in UK](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/clyj4zky4zwo) ## [Sell Chrome to end search monopoly, Google told](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cp81ppr3l9go) ![A green promotional banner with black squares and rectangles forming pixels, moving in from the right. The text says: “Tech Decoded: The world’s biggest tech news in your inbox every Monday.”](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/480/cpsprodpb/41d3/live/348b21e0-26a8-11f0-8f57-b7237f6a66e6.png.webp) [Sign up for our Tech Decoded newsletter](https://www.bbc.co.uk/newsletters/zxh6cxs) to follow the world's top tech stories and trends. But collapse can also be a moment of clarification… if we know where to look.
